description_long: This sculpture is a detailed, classical representation of a draped, headless and armless female figure with large, intricately carved wings extending from the back. The surface is textured to resemble aged marble, with visible folds in the flowing garment and feather details on the wings. The body is slightly twisted, giving a sense of dynamic movement, and the wings are spread wide, suggesting flight or a dramatic pose. The coloration is primarily off-white with brownish patina, enhancing the sense of antiquity and weathering. The base of the sculpture is irregular, as if broken or unfinished, and there are no visible supports or plinths. The overall form and style are reminiscent of Hellenistic Greek statuary, such as the famous Winged Victory of Samothrace.
description: A headless, armless, winged female figure in flowing drapery, carved in a weathered marble style with detailed feathers and garment folds.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the detailed wings, draped garment, and the absence of head and arms.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the layered feathers of the wing and the twisting motion of the torso.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the garment, the spread of both wings, and the full curvature of the figure.
description_view_3: Right side view focusing on the right wing's feather detail and the flowing lines of the dress.
attribute_subject_matter: winged female figure in classical drapery
attribute_location_context: No visible context or environment; background is plain and featureless.
